user_prompt: Draft a Data Processing Agreement template for a small SaaS using a US-based hosting provider.
actual_output:
# Data Processing Agreement (DPA) Template
## SaaS Provider & US-Based Hosting Provider

**Disclaimer:** This template is compliance guidance, not legal advice. Have qualified legal counsel review before execution, particularly regarding liability caps and indemnification provisions, which vary by jurisdiction.

---

## 1. DEFINITIONS

**"Personal Data"** means any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person, as defined in GDPR Article 4(1).

**"Processing"** means any operation performed on Personal Data, as defined in GDPR Article 4(2).

**"Data Controller"** means the entity determining the purposes and means of Processing (the SaaS Provider).

**"Data Processor"** means the entity processing Personal Data on behalf of the Controller (the Hosting Provider).

**"Sub-processor"** means any entity engaged by the Processor to process Personal Data on the Processor's behalf.

**"Data Subject"** means the individual to whom Personal Data relates.

**"Supervisory Authority"** means the competent data protection authority in the EU/EEA.

---

## 2. SCOPE & APPLICABILITY

**2.1 Applicability**
This DPA applies to all Processing of Personal Data by the Hosting Provider on behalf of the SaaS Provider, including:
- Storage of customer data on servers located in [**specify: US region(s)**]
- Backup and disaster recovery operations
- System maintenance and monitoring
- Any other Processing activities specified in Exhibit A (Technical & Organizational Measures)

**2.2 GDPR Compliance**
This DPA incorporates the Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) as approved by the European Commission (Decision 2021/914) to enable lawful transfers of Personal Data from the EU/EEA to the United States. Both parties acknowledge the Schrems II decision and commit to implementing supplementary safeguards as outlined in Section 8.

**2.3 Non-GDPR Data**
To the extent the Hosting Provider processes Personal Data subject to other privacy laws (CCPA, LGPD, etc.), the parties agree to comply with applicable requirements or execute separate addenda.

---

## 3. SUBJECT MATTER & DURATION

**3.1 Subject Matter**
The Processor shall process Personal Data solely:
- For the purposes specified in the underlying Service Agreement dated [**insert date**]
- In accordance with documented instructions from the Controller
- For the duration of the Service Agreement and [**X months**] thereafter for data deletion/return

**3.2 Categories of Personal Data**
- Names, email addresses, phone numbers
- Usage analytics and log data
- [**Add any special category data if applicable**]

**3.3 Categories of Data Subjects**
- End users of the SaaS platform
- [**Add others: employees, contractors, etc.**]

**3.4 Duration**
This DPA commences on [**date**] and continues for the term of the Service Agreement, plus [**X months**] for data deletion/return activities.

---

## 4. PROCESSOR OBLIGATIONS

**4.1 Processing Instructions**
The Processor shall process Personal Data only on documented written instructions from the Controller, including:
- Purpose(s) of Processing
- Categories of Personal Data
- Categories of Data Subjects
- Duration of Processing

The Processor shall not process Personal Data for its own purposes without prior written consent.

**4.2 Confidentiality**
The Processor shall ensure that persons authorized to process Personal Data:
- Are subject to confidentiality obligations
- Have received appropriate training on data protection
- Are bound by written confidentiality agreements

**4.3 Sub-processors — Art. 28(2) & (4)**

**4.3.1 Authorization**
The Processor may engage Sub-processors only with prior written authorization from the Controller. The Controller grants standing authorization for Sub-processors listed in Exhibit B, provided:
- The Processor notifies the Controller of any changes at least 30 days in advance
- The Controller may object to new Sub-processors on reasonable grounds within 15 days
- If the Controller objects, the parties shall negotiate in good faith; if unresolved, the Controller may terminate the affected services without penalty

**4.3.2 Sub-processor Obligations**
The Processor shall impose equivalent data protection obligations on Sub-processors via written contract, including:
- Confidentiality requirements
- Security and audit rights
- Liability for Sub-processor breaches

The Processor remains fully liable to the Controller for Sub-processor performance.

**4.4 Data Subject Rights — Art. 15-22**
The Processor shall:
- Assist the Controller in responding to Data Subject access requests (DSARs) within 10 business days of notification
- Provide requested Personal Data in a structured, commonly used, machine-readable format
- Assist with data portability, deletion, and rectification requests
- Not respond directly to Data Subjects without Controller authorization

**4.5 International Transfers — Art. 44-49**
The Processor acknowledges that transfers of Personal Data to the United States require appropriate safeguards. See Section 8 (Supplementary Safeguards).

---

## 5. CONTROLLER OBLIGATIONS

**5.1 Lawful Basis**
The Controller warrants that it has a lawful basis (Art. 6) for Processing and has obtained necessary consents (Art. 7) or other legal grounds.

**5.2 Instructions**
The Controller shall provide clear, documented Processing instructions and notify the Processor of any changes.

**5.3 Data Subject Rights**
The Controller shall respond to Data Subject requests and cooperate with the Processor in fulfilling rights under Articles 15-22.

---

## 6. SECURITY & TECHNICAL MEASURES — Art. 32

**6.1 Processor Responsibilities**
The Processor shall implement and maintain appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect Personal Data against:
- Unauthorized or unlawful Processing
- Accidental loss, destruction, or damage
- Unauthorized access or disclosure

**6.2 Specific Measures**
The Processor commits to:

| Control | Requirement |
|---------|-------------|
| **Encryption in Transit** | TLS 1.2 or higher for all data transfers |
| **Encryption at Rest** | AES-256 or equivalent for stored data |
| **Access Controls** | Role-based access, multi-factor authentication for admin access |
| **Audit Logging** | Comprehensive logs of all access and modifications, retained for [**X months**] |
| **Vulnerability Management** | Regular penetration testing and vulnerability scans (at least annually) |
| **Incident Response** | Documented procedures; notification to Controller within 24 hours of discovery |
| **Backup & Recovery** | Daily backups, tested recovery procedures, [**X-day**] RTO/RPO |
| **Data Center Security** | Physical access controls, surveillance, environmental monitoring |
| **Personnel Security** | Background checks, confidentiality agreements, data protection training |

**6.3 Compliance Verification**
The Processor shall provide evidence of compliance upon request, including:
- SOC 2 Type II audit reports (or equivalent)
- Penetration test summaries
- Incident logs and remediation records

---

## 7. DATA BREACHES — Art. 33-34

**7.1 Notification Obligation**
The Processor shall notify the Controller **without undue delay and in no case later than 24 hours** after discovering a Personal Data breach, including:
- Nature and scope of the breach
- Likely consequences
- Measures taken or proposed to mitigate harm
- Contact point for further information

**7.2 Cooperation**
The Processor shall:
- Preserve evidence and logs
- Cooperate fully with breach investigations
- Assist the Controller in notifying Supervisory Authorities and Data Subjects
- Provide a detailed breach report within 5 business days

**7.3 No Admission of Liability**
Breach notification does not constitute admission of liability or fault.

---

## 8. SUPPLEMENTARY SAFEGUARDS FOR US TRANSFERS — Schrems II Compliance

**8.1 Acknowledgment of Risks**
Both parties acknowledge that:
- US government surveillance programs (FISA Section 702, Executive Order 12333) may access Personal Data
- The European Court of Justice found the Privacy Shield and standard contractual clauses alone insufficient (Schrems II, C-311/18)
- Supplementary safeguards are required to meet GDPR standards

**8.2 Supplementary Measures**

**8.2.1 Data Minimization**
The Processor shall:
- Process only Personal Data necessary for the specified purposes
- Implement pseudonymization where feasible
- Limit retention to [**X months**] unless longer retention is legally required

**8.2.
judge_reasoning: The chatbot provided a comprehensive and detailed Data Processing Agreement template as requested, adhering to its role as a GDPR Auditor while including the necessary legal disclaimers.
